title: Final Exam Review Session Notes

examFormat.materials: Open notes from this course offering only
examFormat.restrictions: Closed internet, no AI, no materials from other courses
examFormat.partialCredit: Show work in Excel, handwritten scan, or Word

topicSummaries.content:
Capacity Management & Queuing: Single server M, ρ (utilization), coefficient of variation for arrivals/service. Multi-server: More complex formula but reduces to single server when n=1. Utilization = time busy ÷ total time available. Little's Law: WIP = flow rate × throughput time.

Economic Order Quantity (EOQ): Repeated ordering decisions, minimizes inventory costs. Formula: √(2DS/H) where D=demand, S=setup cost, H=holding cost. Holding cost = interest rate × unit cost + physical holding cost. At optimal EOQ: procurement cost = holding cost.

News Vendor Model: One-shot decisions, maximizes revenue. Critical fractile = Cu/(Cu + Co) where Cu=underage cost, Co=overage cost. Use NORM.INV function with critical fractile, mean, standard deviation.

Factory Physics: Bottleneck = stage with least capacity. Cycle time = reciprocal of capacity. Flow rate = minimum of demand and capacity.

Setups - reduce through training, standardization, parallelization. Bottlenecks - can shift as order sizes change, may have multiple. Uncertainty/variability - drives need for buffering vs. 100% utilization.
100% utilization feels efficient but creates vulnerability. Emergency services (fire, medical) intentionally run low utilization. High utilization + variability = dramatic waiting time increases.

Improve Performance: Increase servers/parallelization, decrease arrival/service time variability, appointments reduce arrival variability, training/standardization reduces service variability.

Manage Perceptions: Set proper expectations (DMV example), mirrors near elevators reduce perceived wait time, single line feeding multiple servers feels fairer.

EOQ vs News Vendor Decision Factors: EOQ for repeated orders, basic/non-seasonal items, given cost of capital. News Vendor for one-time orders, seasonal items, given salvage value.

Practice Problem Results: Hat retailer EOQ: 169 units per order (monthly demand 100, setup $50). Seasonal hat News Vendor: 1,041 units (55.6% service level). Annual vs monthly calculations yield same EOQ results.

Bullwhip Effect: Small demand changes create outsized upstream variations. Caused by reactive ordering, poor communication, promotions. Solutions: information sharing, better forecasting, limit order increases.

Shortage Gaming: Customers over-order when expecting partial fulfillment. Counter with order increase limits, cancellation penalties.

Operation Strategy (Class 1): Cost, quality, delivery, flexibility matrix alignment. McDonald's vs Burger King operational differences.

Product-Process Matrix (Class 3): Volume/variety vs flexibility positioning. General Hospital vs Narayana Heart Hospital examples.

Supply Chain Management (Classes 7, 8, 10): Efficient vs responsive supply chain matching. Uncertainty framework and customization strategies.

Risk Management (Class 6): Anticipatory strategies vs reactive adaptations. COVID brewery-to-sanitizer pivot example.

studyTips: Templates strongly recommended for quantitative problems
Can bring hard copy or soft copy notes (no AI during exam)
Download exam, turn off WiFi after download
Excel submission required with answers in designated cells
Showing work is essential since partial credit only awarded when reasoning is clearly demonstrated
Work can be directly in Excel, scanning handwritten work, or copy-pasting from Word
You may use any materials from current course offering only
Practice with all four template types: capacity, queueing, EOQ, newsvendor
Focus on second-half topics: queueing, EOQ, newsvendor, supply chain strategies
Understand the three pain points and how they appear across all topics
Know when to use EOQ vs newsvendor based on problem characteristics
